meta.name: Half Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 1:30 — elite club target over 24 weeks
meta.description: A 24-week elite-level programme for competitive runners targeting sub-90 minutes for the half marathon. Six running days per week, VO2max intervals twice weekly in peak blocks, and a tune-up race at week 16. Includes two gym sessions per week for strength and injury prevention.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-1:30 requires 4:16/km for 21.1km. You need an exceptional lactate threshold and very high VO2max. This plan is designed for runners already regularly covering 60+ km per week. If you are not, build your base first. The tune-up 10k at week 16 is a mandatory checkpoint.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: Opening week. Six running days. Nothing maximal — establish the structure and aerobic feel.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: VO2max block 1
weeks.note: First VO2max session. 1km reps at 4:00/km develop the aerobic ceiling needed for sub-1:30.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Volume peak 1
weeks.note: Volume peak in first block. Long run reaches 22km.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Volume drops. VO2max adaptations consolidate now.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race pace blocks
weeks.note: Race-pace 2km reps. 4:16/km should feel controlled, not frantic.

sessions.det: 20km easy · last 4km at HM race pace

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Dual-quality week
weeks.note: Two quality sessions. This week builds work capacity.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Volume peak 2
weeks.note: Highest volume in the base block. Long run reaches 24km.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Halfway through the base phase. You have built a serious foundation.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Speed sharpening
weeks.note: Short fast reps. 300m at mile pace makes 4:16/km feel comfortable.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Long race-pace run
weeks.note: Race-pace long run section. 8km at 4:16/km under fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Peak block 1
weeks.note: 1600m reps at race pace — the most specific interval session of the plan.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Peak block 2
weeks.note: Second peak week. Long race-pace section builds race confidence.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Peak block 3
weeks.note: Third peak week. Race-pace 1km reps — controlled and confident.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Pre tune-up race deload. You race a 10k next week — arrive fresh.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Tune-up race week — 10k
weeks.note: Tune-up 10k race this weekend. Race it hard — this is a mandatory checkpoint. Your 10k time predicts your half marathon potential. Sub-38:00 suggests sub-1:30 is on track.

sessions.det: 2km WU · Race hard — sub-38:00 is the target for sub-1:30 HM · Use this as a fitness test, not a peak effort

nut.d: Normal race-morning routine. Treat this like a race, not a workout.
Recovery drink or protein shake. Full meal within 90 minutes. Analyse your splits — they tell you exactly where you are.

weeks.title: Post-race rebuild
weeks.note: Bounce back from the 10k. Volume slightly reduced this week — your legs need 5–7 days to fully recover from a race effort.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Final peak week
weeks.note: Final peak before the taper. The 5x1600m session is your last high-intensity stimulus.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Taper prep
weeks.note: Volume reducing. Quality sessions shorter but fast.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Taper 1
weeks.note: First taper week. Volume drops 35%. Intensity maintained.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Taper 2
weeks.note: Second taper week. Fresh legs arriving.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Final prep
weeks.note: Week before race week. Very light.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race week prep
weeks.note: Almost there. Rest well.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90–120 min before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 80 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 min. Protein + carbohydrates essential.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-1:30 attempt
weeks.note: Race week. The fitness is built.

sessions.det: 2km WU · Strides · Race: through 5km at 4:18, settle to 4:16 · Gels at 7km and 14km · Build from 15km · Negative split target

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only foods you have eaten before long runs.
300ml isotonic drink or water. Caffeine if you use it normally.
15min easy jog. 4x100m strides. Full warm-up is essential — you cannot ease into a sub-1:30 half.
Gel with a full cup of water. You have practised this in training.
Second gel with water. This is what separates sub-1:30 athletes from those who fade late.
500ml water or isotonic within 10 minutes. Keep moving gently to cool down.
30g protein immediately. You have just run at high intensity for 90 minutes.
Full meal within 90 minutes. 40g protein, 100g+ carbohydrates. Hydrate consistently through the afternoon.

weeks.title: Race week overflow
weeks.note: Use this week if your race falls on the final Sunday.
